justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model designed with cell-based architecture where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency compliance addressed explicitly. Global dashboard uses anonymized event bridge (PII-stripping ACL) — no cross-region raw data access. Courier assignment regional isolation enforced as domain invariant (courier.regionId must equal order.regionId). Trade-offs explicitly include 3-region operational overhead ('three full deployments to manage, code deployment must be coordinated'). Degraded EU service during outage designed (cached reads, queued writes, no cross-region failover).

observations.text: Exceptionally thorough regional isolation model with cell-based architecture — enforcement at infrastructure level, not just application logic
PII-stripping event bridge as ACL is a strong design for the global dashboard without raw data access
Correctly identifies that cross-region failover violates data residency and designs in-region degraded mode instead
Addresses both GDPR and CCPA explicitly, and notes APAC may have additional requirements (PIPL, DPDP)
Courier regional binding enforced as immutable domain invariant with clear legal rationale
Operational overhead well-articulated: coordinated deployments, no cross-region queries, complex customer migration
Risk analysis includes event bridge PII leakage, GeoDNS misrouting, and dashboard staleness — all realistic failure modes
Phased implementation guidance de-risks the hardest compliance component (event bridge) early

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: Regional isolation model with autonomous cells where no raw data crosses regions. GDPR and CCPA data residency addressed (region assignment by legal jurisdiction, encrypted PII still considered PII). Global dashboard uses aggregated anonymized metrics via Metrics Projector—no cross-region raw data access. Courier-region affinity correctly analyzed as a hard constraint enforced by regional isolation. Trade-offs explicitly cover 3-region operational overhead (deployment drift, IaC templates, sequential CI/CD). Degraded EU service designed with cache-based reads and write-behind queue during outage.

observations.text: Defense-in-depth data residency enforcement across four layers (DNS, gateway, JWT, DB) is excellent—goes beyond the rubric requirement
Metrics Projector as the sole cross-region data bridge with explicit PII stripping is a clean design that clearly satisfies the 'no raw data crosses regions' requirement
GeoDNS misrouting risk identified with JWT region_id validation as mitigation—shows thorough threat modeling
Deployment drift between regional cells called out as high severity risk with concrete mitigation—directly addresses the 3-region operational overhead concern
CCPA explicitly covered via assumption that both GDPR and CCPA prohibit even encrypted PII from leaving jurisdiction
Phased implementation guidance from single-region EU to multi-region is practical and well-sequenced

trials.justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Regional isolation model with independent cells per region, no raw PII crosses borders — enforced structurally via separate VPCs and schema-level PII exclusion. (2) GDPR and CCPA data residency explicitly addressed, with cell-based architecture ensuring data stays in-region. (3) Global dashboard uses anonymized, pre-aggregated metrics via a global metrics bus — no cross-region raw data access. (4) Courier-region invariant enforced at domain level with region-pinned courier pools. (5) Trade-offs extensively cover 3-region operational overhead (IaC management, deployment consistency, no cross-region queries). (6) Degraded EU service during outage designed — EU customers lose write capability but multi-AZ provides partial resilience, with EU dual-cell as Phase 2 evolution.

observations.text: Structural data sovereignty by construction — MetricEvent schema physically lacks PII fields rather than relying on filtering, which is a stronger compliance guarantee than policy-based controls
Defense-in-depth for data residency with three independent layers (GeoDNS, gateway JWT validation, database isolation) preventing misrouted requests from accessing wrong-region data
Comprehensive trade-off analysis with alternatives considered and explicitly rejected with reasoning (e.g., cross-region replication rejected because encryption doesn't satisfy GDPR residency)
Phased migration strategy starting from existing EU deployment avoids big-bang risk
Correctly identifies that traveling customers must still route to home region, not IP-based geolocation
APAC data residency requirements (China PIPL, Japan APPI) acknowledged as low-confidence but not deeply analyzed — could be a gap if APAC cell needs sub-region isolation

A global control plane aggregates pre-computed, anonymized operational metrics for the dashboard without replicating PII across borders.
